Yesterday was a special election day here in Ohio, so I'm on the blog talking about the important 12th Congressional District and last night's results. I'm also discussing yet another shady thing my opponent, Aftab Pureval, has been up to and sharing my first ad! Click below to read more. ### [The Persecution of Christians is Still With Us Today](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-d/)
We tend to think persecuting Christians is something that happened 2000 years ago. But the fact is, it's still going on in some parts of the world to this day. I'm on the blog talking about it today. Click below to read more. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-h/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Are the Democrats Going to Take Back the House This Fall?](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-u/)
The Washington political pundits (virtually all liberals) have been predicting a Democrat takeover for a while now. The most recent to join the chorus is Larry Sabato of the University of Virginia's Center for Politics. He now says Democrats are "soft favorites" to take over the House of Representatives. Click below to read more. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-o/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [Trump and Putin in Helsinki](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jjjtnx-kujkulhluu-n/)
This week on the blog I'm discussing President Trump's meeting in Helsinki with Russian President Vladimir Putin as well as breaking down President Trump's comments following. Click below to read more.

Peter, **My mother, Anne Weills, raised me to be a feminist.** She has fought her entire life for equality for women, in the home and in the workplace, during the civil rights movement and continuing today.**I was taught at a young age to be an advocate for gender equality, and I am committed to continuing this work as a husband, father, and public official.** This week, **I am honored and humbled to receive the endorsement of the National Organization for Women**. NOW is the largest organization of grassroots feminist activists in the country. They are fighting for what I am fighting for: economic justice; racial justice; reproductive justice; LGBTQ rights; ending violence against women; and obtaining equality in pay and job opportunities. **I want to express my thanks to Texas NOW** , which recommended my endorsement after reviewing [my policy commitments](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1855995/9765734/1860643800?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmI5MTQyNy1kMDliLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GkgioMhQH8DbA7k8vGfB0M0C7BXS9TcFqnU1ljXHUVg=). There is so much at stake in this election, and issues of gender equality and justice are among the most important. We need healthcare for all, family leave, sick leave, affordable child care, and more. **I am running against an incumbent who opposed equal pay for women** , who voted to cut healthcare for children and families, who backs the separation of immigrant mothers from their children. He is on the wrong side of history, of course, and women will be the deciding factor in this election. Every day we have new reasons for optimism. **Please continue to support this work by spreading the word,[donating](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1855996/9765735/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmI5MTQyNy1kMDliLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GkgioMhQH8DbA7k8vGfB0M0C7BXS9TcFqnU1ljXHUVg=), and [volunteering](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1855997/9765736/946621143?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmI5MTQyNy1kMDliLWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GkgioMhQH8DbA7k8vGfB0M0C7BXS9TcFqnU1ljXHUVg=)**. body:

Promises he is more often than not quite '"braggadocious" about. **Promises he often does not keep.** Like how he promised to reduce the trade deficit during his time in the White House. **[But so far, it has skyrocketed under his watch.](https://act.myngp.com/el/E96s-yj5Kx6jrvLH7MTi0zAi5DHJsB8uSjolAA66W3c=/DFubNB8Nh0PWgBK2bl5YVBOiOPi0jz8aq-NWARAe5Os=?utm_term=.b857af56a053)** During the Obama years, you couldn't listen to a debate about anything without Washington Republicans screaming about the deficit - but now, with Trump, they're silent. Trump blames this increase in the trade deficit "on very stupid" trade deals with other countries, such as China and Germany. He uses it as an excuse to wage trade wars and impose tariffs, ones that could drive up the cost of every day goods for American consumers. **[But notable economists](https://act.myngp.com/el/E96s-yj5Kx6jrvLH7MTi0zAi5DHJsB8uSjolAA66W3c=/DFubNB8Nh0PWgBK2bl5YVBOiOPi0jz8aq-NWARAe5Os=?utm_term=.b857af56a053)** do not think Trump's tariffs will reduce the trade deficit but rather, put an unnecessary strain on the economy as consumers - who will still want and need these items - will simply end up paying more for them, forcing American companies to either pay more for these items or cede ground to vendors from other nations, not subject to these tariffs. **This will no doubt negatively impact life right here in Florida, our imports and industries and the cost of living for our consumers.** This sort of thing is part of why I decided to throw my hat in the "ring" for the race to become your next Chief Financial Officer (CFO). [Send JR to the cabinet. Contribute $20.18](https://act.myngp.com/el/E96s-yj5Kx6jrvLH7MTi0zAi5DHJsB8uSjolAA66W3c=/wsad3Okmpukh0Z32ontEAkDXqGK2gBcYwk_SH1E1-h4=?am=20.18&recipientId=EsCT1N2SCLZJkIJRRTPlB2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&) The CFO may not have the most glamorous or headline grabbing job - but they are in charge of the state's finances - and making sure that **_YOUR_** hard earned tax payer money is being spent and invested just as our state leadership promised you it would be. **The CFO 's role is one of the most vital checks and balances for our state government - and its interaction with and at the federal level**. As your next CFO, I would make sure that unlike our president, our state is keeping our promises to you, the tax payer and consumer, who keep our state economy running. I am ready to get to work to help make sure your state government is working for **_YOU_**. But I have an election to win first. **And I can 't do it without your help.** [**Can you contribute $20.18 or more today, to help me bring much needed checks and balances to the Cabinet - and help get our state government working for you?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/E96s-yj5Kx6jrvLH7MTi0zAi5DHJsB8uSjolAA66W3c=/wsad3Okmpukh0Z32ontEAkDXqGK2gBcYwk_SH1E1-h4=?am=20.18&recipientId=EsCT1N2SCLZJkIJRRTPlB2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&) Thank you for everything you do, **Jeremy Ring** | POLITICAL ADVERTISEMENT PAID FOR AND APPROVED BY JEREMY RING, DEMOCRAT, FOR CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

subject: The startling facts about America’s prison system

The White House • August 9, 2018
# **The Day Ahead
**
****
* President Donald J. Trump is hosting a roundtable discussion with governors on prison reform and the FIRST STEP Act before Congress.
* Vice President Mike Pence is speaking about the President’s planned Space Force at the Pentagon.

# The startling facts about America’s prison system
**Following successful bipartisan passage** of the FIRST STEP Act in the House of Representatives, President Trump is hosting a roundtable with a number of America’s governors today to discuss implementing prison reform in their states. **President Trump supports efforts to reduce recidivism** —the return of former inmates to prison—as a way to make America’s streets safer. The Administration has worked closely with Congress to find a solution that reduces crime, enhances public safety, and increases opportunity for those who have earned a second chance. **“The facts about America’s prison system are startling,”** Senior Advisor Jared Kushner wrote in The Wall Street Journal in April. “The U.S. has 4% of the world’s population, but roughly 25% of the world’s prisoners. . . . Of the 650,000 people who leave prison every year, two-thirds will commit a new crime within three years.” **The bottom line,** says Kushner: “President Trump promised to fight for the forgotten men and women of this country—and that includes those in prison.” [_The starting facts about America’s prison system._](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA5LjkzNTAxMDYxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwOS45MzUwMTA2MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY3Njc2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&100&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/trumps-forgotten-men-women-include-prisoners/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) **Taking action:** [_President Trump’s principles for reforming our prisons_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA5LjkzNTAxMDYxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwOS45MzUwMTA2MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY3Njc2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&101&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-supports-legislative-action-reduce-recidivism-prison-system/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)
# Vice President Pence on the U.S. Space Force
**In keeping with President Trump’s promise** to rebuild American strength and deterrence, the Administration is moving ahead to fulfill the President’s plan to establish a U.S. Space Force as part of our nation’s Military.

**Vice President Pence,** who chairs the National Space Council—which President Trump revived after a 24-year hiatus—is addressing defense leaders at the Pentagon today about the new Force. His effort builds on the President’s work to reinstate America as a leader in space. **“In America, we understand** that a nation is only living as long as it is striving,” President Trump said in his Inaugural Address. Making America a pioneer in space once again brings us closer to that vision. [_In space, America will once again reach for the Moon—and beyond._](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA5LjkzNTAxMDYxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwOS45MzUwMTA2MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY3Njc2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&102&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/america-will-reach-moon/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) **The facts:** [_President Trump’s “America First” National Space Strategy_](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODA5LjkzNTAxMDYxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgwOS45MzUwMTA2MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzY3Njc2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&103&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-unveiling-america-first-national-space-strategy/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

We can't let a single voter go to the polls believing Scott Wagner's misleading ad.

We know that from his first day in office, Governor Wolf has made education a top priority. He restored the Harrisburg Republicans' $1 billion cut to education and has fought for additional investments in education and workforce development.

Since taking office, Governor Wolf has increased the number of children attending pre-kindergarten by nearly 50 percent, increased the number of students in career and technical education by 42 percent, and increased the high school graduation rate to nearly 90 percent.

But Scott Wagner supported Harrisburg Republicans' $1 billion cut to education. He even called for laying off 10 percent of teachers in Pennsylvania and supported legislation that would strip _billions_ from public education.

We need the resources to fight back against Scott Wagner's gross lies.

body:
Peter -
As you may know, our official fundraising deadline ends at midnight, and it's a big one. So I wanted to send you a quick note before a packed day of campaigning.
After this, we report our numbers for everyone to see, so it's important that we hit our goal for individual donations. The number of people powering this campaign is just as important to me as the total that we raise.
Every new donor and every contribution sends a message about the depth of our grassroots support. Since we don't take corporate money, this is an important way for us to demonstrate that we can beat the political and financial establishment's hand-picked candidates.
If you're able to, it would mean the world to me if you could make a $3 contribution before midnight. Here's the link:
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/deadline](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780ee7/6e822e2f/1a2258cf/41725b60/4282597393/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MDktMS1iIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august-deadline?refcode=em180809-1-b")**

\- Zephyr

subject: A moral compass I relate to
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
Make Maryland progressive again.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37aa0b/7ab0e2c4/7fc7716b/349b1e5c/2190316306/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-castro?refcode=OHQ08098A") Friend, Ben Jealous and I have a lot in common. We both attended demanding schools -- for me it was Stanford and Harvard, for Ben it was Columbia and Oxford -- and followed that up by pounding the pavement to advocate for our communities and vulnerable folks across the country. He may not have a twin brother like I do, but his godbrother is Dave Chappelle, so I don't feel so bad for him. And today, we're both committed to one thing: Bringing progressive change to America. While other folks might be running to the left or to the right, Ben knows the only way forward is running directly to the people. **That's the kind of moral compass I can relate to, which is why I'm so proud to join Team Jealous. He has a tough race this November, but one thing's for sure -- he's not one to back down from a fight.** [**Donate $5 or more now to show you're with Ben in the fight to turn Maryland blue again.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37aa0b/7ab0e2c4/7fc7716b/349b1e5c/2190316306/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-castro?refcode=OHQ08098A") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37aa0b/7ab0e2c4/7fc7716b/349b1e5f/2190316306/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-castro?refcode=OHQ08098A&amount=5&express_lane=true")

---
When I left my position as President Obama's Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, I knew I'd be judged by whether or not our team had created greater opportunity for folks across America -- that included healthy housing, internet access for low-income families and protections for folks who often suffer discrimination.
That's how I knew Ben and I would see eye-to-eye. He's not one to collect trophies in a cabinet -- he's rolling up his sleeves to get things done for folks in Maryland. He wants to make opportunity real for Maryland families, and he knows the only way to do it is to fight right now.
We need to make sure that next year Ben Jealous -- along with his impressive suspenders and socks -- is in the Governor's mansion working on behalf of Marylanders.
**I think Maryland is ready for a progressive leader like Ben, but I need to know you're with me:** [**Let's make opportunity real again for Maryland families -- donate $5 or more now to fund the fight.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37aa0b/7ab0e2c4/7fc7716b/349b1e5c/2190316306/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-castro?refcode=OHQ08098A")
Thanks for everything you do.
In solidarity,
Julian Castro
Former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
Founder of Opportunity First

name: Mark Osmack
subject: Thanks to your hard work...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Osmack for Missouri |
body:
Friends,

I want to thank you. Thank you for your immeasurable dedication. Commitment. Energy. Tirelessness. Enthusiasm. I want to thank you for all this...and more. You have been the campaign. You are the campaign.

27,389. That is how many people voted for us because of you. Each vote. Each vote is a direct result of the doors you knocked. The phone calls you made. The postcards you wrote. The Facebook messages, the tweets. The word of mouth...what you said to friends and family. All these things led to 27,389 people in this District believing in us. I will cherish the trust and confidence that so many have given me over this past year. Thank you, thank you, thank you...sincerely. It has been nothing but an honor.

I am truly in awe of you. If the incredible outpouring of effort and support for grassroots, progressive, and authentic campaigns like ours is the first step we take toward revitalizing our democracy and rooting out the heartless, bottomless corruption that has infected our politics, then we have taken a giant leap toward that goal. Don't ever give up. We will win if we stay true to each other.

My name won't be on the ballot in November, but wonderful leaders will be. Together we can do big things. We can make lasting change. And we can, together, put this country on a path toward justice, fairness, and love.

As for me, I'll be putting all my energy and time into helping Claire McCaskill win. She is a remarkable person and an outstanding leader. Also, I'll also be doing everything I can to help Kevin Fitzgerald, Erica Hoffman, and Mike Revis. We must also coalesce together and send a Democrat to win MO-2. I humbly ask everyone to stay involved, join a campaign that you are passionate about, and let's win up and down the ballot in November!

Always, with the deepest respect and gratitude,

-Mark

body:
The story in this ad is personal. It's about my dad, whose career in law enforcement inspired me to enter public service and defend our country in the CIA.
| | Peter, I hope you had a chance to watch [**our first television ad**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WS148G-oN6QuJh8c_jhUfEDpdtCW6BvxgB8TE3k9pks=/NS2Tm0rD2x9cM7fIHk4Z8yvF5cVzZQ3fQlUCCmkkIBY=?refcode=em_fr_2018.08.09_b1_as_all&recurring=auto&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) of the general election campaign. This ad is special to me. It's about my dad, whose career in law enforcement inspired me to enter public service and defend our country in the CIA. My dad taught me to value service from a young age -- and it's that value that defines this campaign. With this ad, we'll continue to share our campaign's values with families across our district. [**But I 'm depending on this group of supporters to help sustain our efforts, so if you could make a donation of any amount after you watch, I would really appreciate it.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WS148G-oN6QuJh8c_jhUfEDpdtCW6BvxgB8TE3k9pks=/NS2Tm0rD2x9cM7fIHk4Z8yvF5cVzZQ3fQlUCCmkkIBY=?refcode=em_fr_2018.08.09_b1_as_all&recurring=auto&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) Thank you, Peter, for all you do for this campaign. We will win this race together. \- Abigail \-----forwarded message----- From: Dana Bye <dana@abigailspanberger.com> Date: Wed, Aug 8, 2018 at 6:11 PM Subject: we're up on TV! Friends, Today is a big day for our campaign in Virginia's 7th district: _**We 're up on TV with our first ad of the general election!**_ This ad, "Values," is really special, so I hope you can [**take a look.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WS148G-oN6QuJh8c_jhUfEDpdtCW6BvxgB8TE3k9pks=/NS2Tm0rD2x9cM7fIHk4Z8yvF5cVzZQ3fQlUCCmkkIBY=?refcode=em_fr_2018.08.09_b1_as_all&recurring=auto&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) It's Abigail's story -- **her upbringing, her values, and her commitment to serving our country, first in the CIA and next as a representative for families in the 7th district.** [**Please, watch the ad, and then consider making a donation to help us share Abigail 's story with voters across our district:**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WS148G-oN6QuJh8c_jhUfEDpdtCW6BvxgB8TE3k9pks=/NS2Tm0rD2x9cM7fIHk4Z8yvF5cVzZQ3fQlUCCmkkIBY=?refcode=em_fr_2018.08.09_b1_as_all&recurring=auto&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/WS148G-oN6QuJh8c_jhUfEDpdtCW6BvxgB8TE3k9pks=/NS2Tm0rD2x9cM7fIHk4Z8yvF5cVzZQ3fQlUCCmkkIBY=?refcode=em_fr_2018.08.09_b1_as_all&recurring=auto&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) Families in our district deserve a representative in Congress who will put their needs first and work to solve the problems affecting Central Virginia families. body:

Friend -- I’ve spent my life fighting against corruption in business and politics. As a law professor at the University of Minnesota, I specialize in corporate finance law and professional responsibility. I served as Chief Ethics Counsel in the White House, and have written several books calling for campaign finance reform and reforms of our financial sector.

In this election, the issues matter and it’s important to know where the candidates stand.

I stand for:

**The environment:**
It is a travesty that the GOP has turned their back on environmental protection. It is even more distressing that my opponent Tina Smith has done so as well.

• I oppose the proposed PolyMet project in Northern Minnesota, and will introduce legislation to ban any toxic mining sulfide near waterways nationwide.
• I support imposing a sensible carbon tax to reduce pollution and create revenue for environmental conservation programs.
• I support re-entering the Paris Climate Accords and working towards a global solution to climate change.

**Healthcare:**
I believe that delivering affordable, efficient healthcare is one of the most important issues facing our country. I support a single-payer, Medicare-for-All solution.

**Abolishing corruption in Washington:**
We need to hold Donald Trump and his administration accountable. In the Senate, I will call for immediate impeachment hearings of President Trump and Vice President Pence, and if warranted by the evidence, removal from office. My opponent Tina Smith opposes such steps because, in her words, Trump is still popular in parts of Minnesota.

**On election reform:**
I am the only candidate in this race who refuses to accept PAC money. I believe that PAC contributions are legalized bribes, and I want to overturn Citizens United and pass sensible campaign finance laws.

You can view my other policy positions at www.painterminnesota.com

I want to bring responsibility and ethics back to Washington. **Can you please help us today with a contribution to help us get to the finish line? Every dollar makes a difference.**

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: DeVos to cut $13 BILLION from student debt relief [take action]
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | _"Betsy DeVos proposes rules that would**cut student loan relief by an estimated $13 billion"**_
\-- _LA Times_ ; 7/25/2018 A college education is already too expensive for many students. Every year, many students graduate with debilitating debt, causing them to put off life goals. **Peter, Education Secretary Betsy DeVos is trying to make it even harder for college graduates to succeed.** DeVos is proposing a rule that will make it harder for people to gain relief after they're hit with massive student loans. It's a move that lines the pockets of for-profit college executives at the expense of hardworking students. She's putting profits ahead of our students' educations. [**Join me and tell DeVos: Students deserve better.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ff88f/4462dc26/6f7d5416/20075a16/2016691418/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e180809m") Students are already struggling to afford the cost of tuition and living in college. New studies show that around 40% of students work in addition to attending school. About one in four are parents. Many struggle to even afford adequate nutrition. And even though costs and fees keep rising, financial aid has not kept pace. A college education is practically necessary in today's global economy. Any student with college ambitions should be able to afford it -- without struggling with overwhelming debt. Betsy DeVos is supposed to be working for our students, not special interests. But right now, it's the other way around. Help me remind her where her priorities should lie. [**If you agree that our students deserve better, add your name to mine right now.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ff88f/4462dc26/6f7d5416/20075a16/2016691418/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e180809m") With gratitude, Sherrod

But democracy's not a spectator sport, and we've got to get in the game. Just last month, I was in Georgia helping Stacey reach out to voters - because this race is that important. Stacey will need all hands on deck, and I encourage you to join her campaign as a volunteer. Whether or not you can spare that time - and I truly hope you can - I encourage you to say loud and clear that "I'm in" by making a contribution today. name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: I’ve never been more certain of this
body:
Peter,
As part of _My Faith Votes_ ' plan to reach 51 million people of faith and get nonvoters to the polls, we are now digging even deeper to reach a couple groups that are often overlooked at our own peril – Millennials and Generation Z.
Combined, Millennials and Generation Z represent 69.2 million eligible voters, the largest voting bloc in U.S. history, and yet they are the generations with the lowest voter participation.
**It is imperative that we get these young people off the sidelines and standing for biblical values, and here's how we can do it...**
_My Faith Votes_ is once again partnering with a group called im2moro to inspire and equip nearly 1 million young students to vote in November. Specifically, we will reach 300,000 students on 100 Christian college campuses in targeted states, and at least another 600,000 through their social media and mobile connections.
With these students living away from home, we will use proven technology to simplify the process for each student to get registered and successfully cast an absentee ballot.
**[I'm told this initiative will cost approximately $60,000 and the good news is that a very generous donor has committed to match at least $10,000 of what we're able to raise online. Will you help us reach our goal with a tax-deductible donation right now](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180809apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180809apl01ndb&n=1)?**
If you're wondering if your donation will make a difference, let me share something incredible with you.
In 2016, we ran this campaign on 41 Christian university campuses. We reached 150,000 students, and **73% of those students who used our tools voted in the election!** That is near twice the voting rate of their peers!
And most of their peers aren’t voting for biblical values.
Let me ask you...have you heard of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez? She is the 28-year-old self-described socialist millennial who beat a Democratic incumbent Congressman in the New York Congressional primary earlier this year.
She has drawn national attention to her campaign because of her extreme socialist views, and many are asking, “is there hope for the next generation of politicians? Can they overcome a socialist mentality?”
The answer is “YES!”
**_My Faith Votes_ has a proven plan to engage the next generation on Christian University Campuses across the nation, to be defenders of our values in the public square!**
And that’s why we’re more than doubling our efforts from 2016 in a commitment to turn these young Christians into lifelong voters who will vote from a biblical worldview.
This is not a theory. We have proven results. Now it's time to put our resources to work once again in order to get these young voters off the sidelines in 2018, and I have no doubt whatsoever that we can get it done.
**[Peter, thanks to the generous matching grant, your donation will be doubled up to $10,000! Please help us reach our financial goal with a tax-deductible donation now. We're itching to get to work and deliver another victory for our Judeo-Christian values](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180809apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180809apl01ndb&n=2).**
Thank you so much for your ongoing support, we truly cannot do this without you.
[](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180809apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180809apl01ndb&n=3)
**United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180809apl01ndb&n=4)
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180809apl01ndb&n=5).

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Not counting our chickens. Getting to work.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
A blue wave is coming to our country and to Washington.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins") Friend, In 2018, no one is counting their chickens before they hatch. With so much at stake, we're not going to back down -- not from a single fight, not in any corner of our country. **That's why Tu esday was so important, and so worth celebrating. ** In **Ohio** , we're within inches of taking back a House seat that's been red for 35 years. In **Michigan** , they're ready to elect the first-ever Muslim woman to Congress. As the chair of the DGA, I couldn't be more excited that Gretchen Whitmer is poised to flip the Wolverine state blue! In**Kansas** , primary-winner Sharice Davids could become the first Native American woman elected to Congress, and the first LGBTQ legislator from her state. In **Missouri** , voters took a stand against a terrible anti-union measure. And primary night in **Washington**? We made it clear Washington state would not stand by silently as Donald Trump and his party attack all we hold dear! [**Tuesday night was a great night for Democrats -- and for our progressive movement. But we can't rest on our laurels -- we must seize this moment and we must win in November. Help us carry the energy we saw in the primaries through to the general election: Chip in right now.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins") Here in Washington, we're seeing major victories at every level. In the state house, we saw a huge wave and Democrats now hold an advantage in 16 (16!) seats that are currently held by Republicans. In the state senate, Democrats are ahead in four Republican-held seats. We currently have a slim one-seat majority in the senate, but come November we can grow it. That would be a huge boost for our goal of passing the most aggressive progressive agenda in the nation. This is huge for Washington -- and for our country. We can cement our ability to lead the nation in progressive policies, and continue to show Republicans why our progressive state has the #1 economy in the country. [**Will you propel our movement right now?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins") | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins")
---
We are also doing our part to take back the United States House. **Washington is going to be the epicenter of the fight.** Our two competitive congressional races in Washington state became three Tuesday night. In the 8th, Dino Rossi -- who has nearly universal name ID -- is tracking far behind the total votes of Democrats across the district. In the 5th, Lisa Brown, a champion for working families, is neck and neck with Republican incumbent Cathy McMorris Rodgers, who's voted with Donald Trump 97.8% of the time. And in the 3rd, Democrat Carolyn Long is showing huge momentum in a district that's been a GOP stronghold for years.
A blue wave is coming to our country and to Washington. We MUST expand our majority in the state legislature. We MUST flip Congress by sending 3 new Democrats to D.C. And we WILL stop Trump's agenda, here in the states and nationwide.
[**But to do it, we WILL need your help to keep this momentum going -- chip in right away!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins")
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
[CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd786b8/2f5ea88d/76ee2df3/2cbc30e3/1497292870/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-carpe-momentum?refcode=ema_2018-08-09_primary-wins")

subject: Back on the Right Track
body:
We have taken many positive and measurable steps forward in just 19 months, and we’re on the right track. |

| Friend,
---
| Every village, town and city in Vermont can have a thriving economy. We can have an economy consistently growing faster than the costs of living, so our state is more affordable, and each year families can be more secure. We can have the best education system in the nation, where every school offers a full range of 21st Century educational opportunities and every child graduates prepared to compete and succeed. And with a thriving economy, we can invest in a cleaner environment, safer neighborhoods with fewer suffering from addiction, and meet our obligation to the most vulnerable.

I ran for Governor because I believe we can achieve this vision with leadership that is honest about the scope of our challenges, sets clear priorities to address them, is willing to put progress ahead of the politics and policies of the past – and is able to do the hard work and make the difficult decisions while encouraging civility and respect.

For years our population growth has been slowing, and a shrinking working-age population resulted in an average of 6 fewer workers in our workforce and 3 fewer kids in our schools every single day. State spending and tax burdens were rising faster than many Vermonters’ pay, and there was too little focus on restoring the economic and fiscal fundamentals required to invest in our future. That’s why my Administration has had the courage to tackle the tough issues, like getting spending growth under control, strengthening our education system, eliminating waiting lists for opioid treatment and fighting to give Vermonters a break from increasing taxes and fees.

While we have much more work to do, in my first 19 months in office we have put Vermont on a better path. And, we are making measurable progress with real results.

State government is now living within its means. Better management and more economic activity have combined to generate a $70 million budget surplus across all funds. Spending growth over the 5 fiscal years before I took office averaged almost 5 percent per year, funded largely by unsustainable tax increases. Now, spending is growing at just 2 percent, without raising taxes or fees – and our surplus should allow us to return more money to taxpayers in the future, if the Legislature agrees.

We are modernizing state government to make it more efficient and productive. By consolidating IT operations within an Agency of Digital Services, for example, we saved about $2.2 million. We’ve reduced State operating costs by $32.5 million, while improving the delivery of services. And the Agency of Human Services, alone, closed out this fiscal year having spent $70 million less, across all funds, than the last fiscal year of the previous Administration. That’s over $100 million taxpayers have not had to pay – without reducing frontline services.

We also prevented about $71 million in forecasted statewide property tax rate increases and held residential property tax rates level for two years in a row. If you are a residential property tax payer and your bill went up, it’s due to local factors, not the statewide rate. And I want to be clear: I am insisting we address education because I believe we can transform our education system from good, to the very best in the country. This requires the courage to reduce inefficiency and invest the savings in more, and better, opportunities for kids.

While we grappled with K-12 reform, we also increased investment in career technical education, workforce training and higher education by about $5 million, and childcare assistance for working families by $2.5 million.

We passed the largest housing investment in state history, which will generate hundreds of new housing units working families can afford, 1,000 construction jobs and $100 million in construction activity. We also streamlined the regulatory process so more housing for working families can be built and cost less.

Over the last two years, we’ve cut the cost of workers compensation insurance by over 10 percent, saving employers an estimated $30 million. This year, we cut income tax rates across the board and eliminated income taxes on social security benefits for low and middle-income retirees, saving Vermonters $30 million. And, while we still have more to do to expand our workforce, there are now 4,500 more Vermonters in the private sector labor force.

We have taken many positive and measurable steps forward in just 19 months, and we’re on the right track. But we still have much more work ahead. If we stay focused on making Vermont’s economy stronger, and our state more affordable for families and business, Vermont will be a state where every community and every family can thrive and prosper.

Sincerely,

Phil Scott

**P.S. - If you agree that we need to continue our work to make Vermont more affordable,_please vote for me on, or before, August 14th in the Republican Primary_. I would appreciate your support!**

Dan Torres

name: Team Scholten
subject: TBT - Steve King only one to vote against recognizing slave labor’s construction of U.S. Capitol
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
Our campaign has been affectionately referred to by many as a “throwback campaign.” From our retro-looking logo, to campaigning across the district in an R.V., we’ll happily wear this label with pride. In fact, J.D. has said many times:
_“I may be a younger candidate, but I have an old-school mentality. I’ve modeled my campaign after two of my political heroes, Rep. Berkley Bedell and Sen. Tom Harkin. What I learned from them is that if you get out and meet the people, show that you can be trusted, and prove that you will fight for them—you can earn their vote.”_
We spend most of our time talking about what J.D. stands for, but in the spirit of a “throwback” campaign, we’re going to occasionally use “throwback Thursday” as a day to remind you about some of the divisive things Steve King has done over the years.

**Today, we’re taking a trip back to 2009** , when Congress passed a simple resolution to recognize the role that slaves played in the construction of the U.S. Capitol Building by acknowledging them with a marker at the building. **The bill passed, of course, by a vote of 399-1.** Guess who the one person in Congress was that would vote against something like this? Yep, the one and only Steve King!

For far too long, Steve King has gotten away with dividing and distracting us from the real problems facing this country. It’s time for change. It’s time for J.D. Peter,

2018 isn't just another Year of the Woman.

It's the Year of the EMILY's List Woman.

We've already endorsed hundreds of pro-choice Democratic women running for office this year. We have enough women to single-handedly flip the House and Senate. And we can grow the number of pro-choice Democratic women governors in the country up from just two.

THIS is what we mean when we say we can change the face of power for years to come.

**[Donate $3 to help make 2018 the biggest year for pro-choice Democratic women yet.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c483c/24c69fd9/2609b6098/18845f9d/108323595/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180809_email_YearofELWoman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+make+2018+the+biggest+y&utm_campaign=WQEFET25N_digital_email_20180809_YearofELWoman_inact&source=WQEFET25N_digital_email_20180809_YearofELWoman_inact")**

Our women have already made headlines during the first half of this year by dominating their primary elections, including our 31 women who delivered victories this week.

It's not hard to see why: Voters are excited about our candidates. They want to elect them because they know they can trust our women to tirelessly fight for them once in office.

But there are no guarantees. Our women are running in tough races. And Republican super PACs are already spending millions to defeat them.

Luckily, that's where we come in.

Our women count on having this strong community behind them to help them win on Election Day.

That's how we make history.

**Donate $3 now to help ensure 2018 is the Year of the EMILY's List Woman:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Make-This-Our-Year](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c483c/24c69fd9/2609b6098/18845f9e/108323595/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180809_email_YearofELWoman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgMakeThisOurYear&utm_campaign=WQEFET25N_digital_email_20180809_YearofELWoman_inact&source=WQEFET25N_digital_email_20180809_YearofELWoman_inact")**

Congressman Lou Barletta has railed against the Affordable Care Act in the House and on the campaign trail, and now he's refusing to stand up for people with pre-existing conditions.

[**_Americans can't go back to wondering if they'll lose coverage just because they have a pre-existing condition. Will you join Bob Casey today in fighting for health care coverage?_**](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180809_fr1_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

In an interview yesterday, Barletta refused to stand up for the millions of Pennsylvanians who will suffer if the Trump administration successfully allows insurance companies to once again discriminate against people with pre-existing conditions. Barletta has repeatedly voted to repeal the ACA, and even campaigns on the fact that, if he had been in the Senate last year, he would have been the final vote the GOP needed to end the ACA for good.

[**_We can't let that happen. Can you chip in $5 to help Bob fight for health care protections?_**](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180809_fr1_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

body:
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc48ed/d0ce7de/511e5cf9/9cd373f/3790081456/VEsH/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc48ed/d0ce7de/511e5cf9/9cd373f/3790081456/VEsE/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
Dear friends,
Throughout my life, I've championed women's reproductive rights.
So when news broke yesterday that Planned Parenthood New Hampshire Action Fund endorsed me for governor of New Hampshire, it was a big deal.
Planned Parenthood is on the front lines for women in New Hampshire and across our country to make sure everyone has access to the health care they need - from cancer screenings to LGBTQ services to reproductive health care.
I share their commitment, and I'm proud and honored to have their endorsement.
Now is a critical time for Planned Parenthood and women's reproductive rights.
**It 's been a generation since we've faced this big of a threat to reproductive rights. [Chip in $5, or whatever you can, to protect reproductive rights in the Granite State.](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc48ed/d0ce7de/511e5cf9/9cd373e/3790081456/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2VsbHlmb3Jnb3Zlcm5vcmVtYWlscyIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoianRrOTAtZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kellyforgovernoremails?refcode=jtk90-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
With Brett Kavanaugh, Donald Trump's Supreme Court nominee, on the cusp of a lifetime appointment, we're facing unprecedented attacks on a woman's right to access a safe, legal abortion. And Chris Sununu is supporting Kavanaugh anyway.
As governor, I won't allow women's reproductive rights to go backward. Every woman, regardless of income, will continue to have access to a safe, legal abortion as long as I'm in the corner office.
**Now I need your help to unseat a Republican governor to protect reproductive rights for Granite Staters.** **[Will you contribute $5, or whatever you can, to protect a woman's right to choose?](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc48ed/d0ce7de/511e5cf9/9cd373e/3790081456/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2VsbHlmb3Jnb3Zlcm5vcmVtYWlscyIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoianRrOTAtZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kellyforgovernoremails?refcode=jtk90-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
Thank you,
Molly Kelly
[Contribute](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc48ed/d0ce7de/511e5cf9/9cd373e/3790081456/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2VsbHlmb3Jnb3Zlcm5vcmVtYWlscyIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoianRrOTAtZnItbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kellyforgovernoremails?refcode=jtk90-fr-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

body:
Peter - We're so close.
#

Peter -
In case you missed it, we're bumping this morning's email from Zephyr to the top of your inbox. We have just a few hours left until our midnight deadline.
Here's where we're at: We're still short of our goal, but we're close. In fact, we're so close that, if you chip in right now, you could be the one to push us over the top.
Can you use this link and make a $3 donation to help us get there?
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/deadline](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780eef/6e822e2f/1a16f47b/41725970/2529153167/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MDktMy1iIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august-deadline?refcode=em180809-3-b")**
Thank you for being part of this. We are overwhelmed by the grassroots support we've received so far, and when we win, it will be because folks like you made it possible.
All our best,
Team Zephyr

* * *

Peter -
As you may know, our official fundraising deadline ends at midnight, and it's a big one. So I wanted to send you a quick note before a packed day of campaigning.
After this, we report our numbers for everyone to see, so it's important that we hit our goal for individual donations. The number of people powering this campaign is just as important to me as the total that we raise.
Every new donor and every contribution sends a message about the depth of our grassroots support. Since we don't take corporate money, this is an important way for us to demonstrate that we can beat the political and financial establishment's hand-picked candidates.
If you're able to, it would mean the world to me if you could make a $3 contribution before midnight. Here's the link:
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/deadline](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780eef/6e822e2f/1a16f47b/41725970/2529153167/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MDktMy1iIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august-deadline?refcode=em180809-3-b")**
Thank you again for all you do. We're now five weeks away from Election Day, and if we hit this goal, we'll be one step closer to victory.
\- Zephyr

* * *
